title: Habenaria khasiana
taxonomy: Habenaria khasiana is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Within this family, it falls under the subfamily Orchidoideae. Further classification places it in tribe Orchideae and subtribe Orchidinae.
uses: Habenaria khasiana is known for its potential medicinal uses. Research suggests that the plant has antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating various infections. The plant's extracts have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, making it a promising remedy for various health issues. Additionally, Habenaria khasiana is believed to possess hepatoprotective properties, which can help protect the liver from damage. Further studies are needed to confirm the plant's full range of medicinal applications and potential benefits.

intro: Habenaria khasiana, also known as the Himalayan Habenaria, is a species of orchid native to the Himalayan region. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Habenaria khasiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Habenaria khasiana is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
